title: Is it Allowed to Euthanize A Sick Pet?
stanceSummary: Yasir Qadhi addresses "Is it Allowed to Euthanize A Sick Pet" and clarifies the ruling with its limits, evidence, and real-world application. He emphasizes following foundational Islamic principles while recognizing context, hardship, and scholarly nuance when applying the ruling.
alternateQuestions: Is it Allowed to Euthanize A Sick Pet?
Is is it allowed to euthanize a sick pet halal or haram in Islam?
What is the Islamic ruling on is it allowed to euthanize a sick pet?
What does Yasir Qadhi say about is it allowed to euthanize a sick pet?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020-10-21 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=za8gCMOsgvQ) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=za8gCMOsgvQ&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi explains the ruling, key conditions, and practical guidance for this question.

- **Question:** Is it Allowed to Euthanize A Sick Pet?
- **Core approach:** He grounds the answer in Quran, Sunnah, and established fiqh principles.
- **Practical nuance:** He separates broad legal principles from edge cases and modern contexts.

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### Question and Context — [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=za8gCMOsgvQ&t=0)

uh sister iran from england says my cat has been diagnosed with a type of cancer and the vets are saying to euthanize him for his own good i think our sisters perhaps a young sister she's saying i love him a lot and i want to be with him in jannah but i also want what is best for him so am i allowed to euthanize him and will i be with my cat in jannah so our sister iram is asking the painful question of euthanization and then the theological question about jenna and pets in jannah one in [Music] the response to.

this question is that it is completely forbidden in islam to torture animals for no reason it is haram it is sinful to torture animals for no reason we all know the hadith of the lady who went to jahannam because she starved her cat to death for no reason other than she was a nasty person okay and uh our prophet salallahu alaihi wasallam cursed the one who tortures an animal our prophet sallallahu said that anyone who takes an animal as a target practice this is something that is sinful and haram we do not tie up an animal like it is done.

or even like is done in some countries they do a bull fight and they you know kill the animal for entertainment this is completely haram and it is one of the major sins of islam and our prophet salallahu that whoever kills even a small chick a little bird even a small bird or much less a bigger animal without just cause allah subhanahu wa ta'ala will question him on the day of judgment about that it's a threat to that he will have to answer to allah directly for killing an animal without any just cause they said oh messenger of allah what.

is a just cause he says that if he wants to eat an animal he may sacrifice it and then you know eat it he should not just kill it and then let it on the side of the road for entertainment that's not something that is allowed.

### Core Ruling and Evidence — [2:27](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=za8gCMOsgvQ&t=147)

to do so the hadith is very clear that any animal that you're going to kill there must be a reason to kill it right based on this you are saying that your cat is basically suffering is going to die a very slow and miserable death because of cancer and the doctors are saying to euthanize it in this case there is a reason you are not torturing you are not wanting to inflict pain the sanctity of an animal is not like the sanctity of a human this is the key point here right we are not allowed to use this ruling on.

a human being because that is murder a human's life is not the same in the eyes of allah as that of an animal we do not bring about life and death for human beings we leave it to allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala there is no mercy killing when it comes to human beings however when it comes to animals they do not occupy the same level of karma of sanctity of nobility as that of human beings and if the owner of the animal out of mercy for the animal wishes to get rid of the misery of the animal and take the life of.

the animal then the sharia or i should say the majority of scholars give that right i know there's always dissenting voices but the majority of scholars would give that right because it is the right of the animal owner to take the the life of the animal for a legitimate cause uh our shaykh he said in his fatawa that an animal if it becomes sick and it is not one of those animals that you're allowed to eat let's say a donkey and you are not thinking that it's going to be cured there is no sin if you uh kill the animal.

because to keep the animal alive is actually uh spending extra time and money and it's going to be painful to the animal there's no point in doing that so if an animal is killed that is terminally sick there is no sin in this and as for the.

### Practical Guidance and Caveats — [4:25](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=za8gCMOsgvQ&t=265)

animal that can be eaten then it is permissible to actually expedite of course in this case obviously in the cow is slightly sick or whatnot you will expedite and then eat the animal as long as the the meat is not going to be obviously uh spoiled or rotten because of that disease and of course other scholars as well the famous maliki scholar at dardeer he says in his he said that it is allowed to sacrifice that which is not eaten such as a donkey or a mule if it falls sick and it is about to die then it is allowed.

to do that in fact he says it is encouraged to do that in order to save the pain on that animal this is a classical scholar in you know medieval islam and he is saying that when the animal is sick and it's about to die it's in pain it is recommended to get it out of its misery and to expedite this process and so insha'allah you are encouraged in this case the doctors have said that it has cancer so you are encouraged in this case to eliminate it from its cancer and you know these days they have such a humane.

way of euthanizing it's not like in those days in those days it was a knife and that's how they would get rid of it in these days it's uh much more humane and the animal will not feel any pain and ask for your question about jannah dear sister in islam our young sister in islam you work to get to jannah and inshaallah when you are in jannah they will have whatever they want in jannah so all of your desires and all of your hopes and all of your wishes will be satisfied in jannah and this animal of yours will be.

with you uh the goal is we have to make it to jannah we have to work to to get there may allah make us of the people of jannah and with this we come to the conclusion of today's q a and we'll continue next week [Music] he.
